mechanism_narrative: A rising three-month trend in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls rose from April 2020 trough to December 2020
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive 3-month payroll changes are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6 percent, elevated labor slack triggers expectations of sustained monetary accommodation and fiscal support that compress equity risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 55 percent during triggered periods (UNRATE > 6.0) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales and rising industrial production confirms a broad demand-supply expansion without bottleneck constraints, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the marginal effect of either series alone.
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and INDPRO rise, the joint signal produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal impending demand exhaustion and inventory accumulation, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency falls below 55 percent during months when INDPRO declines within late-cycle periods, or Rank IC turns positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate tightens short-term liquidity conditions and raises borrowing costs for leveraged market participants, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the three-month moving average of real retail sales below its twelve-month moving average signals consumer demand deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency falls below 55 percent during triggered periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and falling unemployment create stagflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy sooner than priced by markets, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling UNRATE produces a Rank IC above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating the hypothesized negative relationship fails.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend signals robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-trend GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
